behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local lint logic over prompt-template text (and a provided forbidden-instruction set). MOCK-MISS: this behavior is about one checker’s detection rule, not cross-module handoff. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none required. Rejection-gate: Integration/System/Agentic are disqualified by the behavior text 'static prompt-template lint check' (no requirement for real harness assembly, OS/network/subprocess, or model output).
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test the lint rule function with template strings containing copied phase instructions and near-miss variants; assert it rejects true leakage and allows non-leaking text.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module boundaries: template + interpolation + fixture loading + assembly. MOCK-MISS: a unit test of template text alone can miss leakage introduced during interpolation. Integration-brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess dependency beyond normal local wiring, (b) no AI output dependence, (c) no multi-agentic chain. MEMORY-SLOTS-HYBRID: Evidence: behavior explicitly says 'fully assembled prompt' and 'real test harness'. Boundary signal: leakage can be introduced post-template by assembler modules. Adjacent-lower miss-proof: unit lint of raw template would pass while assembled output leaks.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt-construction path with production-like fixtures and interpolation inputs, then assert assembled prompt has no phase-instruction snippets copied from SKILL.md.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: Behavior claims validation of model behavior being caused by loaded skill context, which depends on real model decisions using that context. Deterministic stubs can verify loading/routing but cannot verify context-driven behavior quality. Integration-brake: (b) yes, correctness depends on real AI output; upgrade to Agentic. Rejection-gate: Workflow is disqualified because the behavior text does not require multiple agentic stages, only one invocation-level behavior validation.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a live model call via the real invocation path that loads skill files; keep user-style prompt free of hard-coded phase rules; assert returned behavior matches the expected context-driven structure/outcome attributable to loaded skill content.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is harness configuration/data wiring: swapping skill-file variant while keeping prompt constant. This is multi-module local orchestration, not inherently model-quality validation. Integration-brake: (a) no required real network/subprocess; local file variants can be exercised in-process, (b) behavior does not require judging model output correctness, (c) no multi-agentic chain. Rejection-gate: Agentic is disqualified by behavior text focusing on 'can run ... variant without changing test prompt text' (execution setup capability).
behaviors.test_description: Wire the harness to two skill-context sources (baseline and degraded/missing SKILL.md) and run identical user prompt text through both execution setups; assert prompt text is unchanged and the harness successfully routes each run to the intended skill variant.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly tests outcome sensitivity to skill-content degradation under constant prompt text; failure depends on what the real model produces from changed context. MOCK-HIDE: stubbed outputs can be fixed to pass and hide true regression. Integration-brake: (b) yes AI output dependence; must be Agentic. Rejection-gate: Workflow is disqualified because behavior specifies a single behavioral test outcome change, not a multi-agentic pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model behavioral test twice with identical user-style prompt: once with intact SKILL.md and once with degraded/removed section; assert pass on intact context and fail on degraded context.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is ordering across modules (model client -> capture artifact -> parser/extractor) plus local artifact persistence. Unit-testing parser alone misses sequencing bugs. Integration-brake: no required real external infra beyond local wiring/filesystem and no dependence on model semantic quality. Rejection-gate: System is disqualified because behavior text does not require real network/subprocess; ordering can be validated with in-process stubs and local artifact sink.
behaviors.test_description: Use the real harness pipeline with a deterministic model stub response; assert raw response artifact is written first and contains unparsed payload, then verify parsing/extraction occurs afterward (via timestamps/events/call order).

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: Core failure boundary is assertion logic correctness (schema-path and field-placement validation) within one local validator. No real infra or model output quality required to prove structural-check rigor. Rejection-gate: Integration is disqualified because behavior text is about assertion semantics themselves, not cross-module handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test assertion helpers against crafted payloads: keyword-present-but-structurally-wrong cases must fail; structurally-correct cases pass even with varied wording.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires live model execution and regression detection based on actual model outputs; this depends on non-deterministic model behavior and cannot be validated by fixed fixtures alone. Integration-brake: (b) yes, correctness depends on real AI output. Rejection-gate: Workflow is disqualified because behavior describes one live execution/assertion stage, not multiple agentic stages in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model tests on prompts known to produce occasional format/placement drift; compare outcomes of structural assertions vs keyword-only checks and assert structural checks fail on real regressions that keyword checks pass.
